title: Marketing Analytics
text:
Below are selected projects that highlight my experience applying marketing analytics to real-world challenges. These projects reflect my ability to combine statistical tools, campaign data, and strategic thinking to inform decisions and drive results.
Whether developing dashboards, analyzing campaign performance, or uncovering insights through SEO and web analytics, each project is rooted in turning complex marketing data into clear, actionable strategies.

title: Marketing Analytics
text:
What You’ll Find

Campaign Performance Analysis: Evaluating the effectiveness of digital marketing efforts using data.
Data Visualization: Turning raw datasets into intuitive dashboards and reports.
Marketing Mix & Attribution: Exploring how different channels contribute to success.
Customer & Market Insights: Using analytics to better understand user behavior and optimize experiences.

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
[Project Title 1: Strategic Campaign Planning]
In this project, I led the development of a comprehensive marketing campaign for [product, service, or initiative]. From audience segmentation to messaging strategy, I managed the full lifecycle of the campaign, aligning it with business objectives and measurable KPIs.
Skills Demonstrated:

Marketing Strategy: Defined objectives and tactics for maximum impact.
Team Coordination: Oversaw collaboration across creative, data, and channel teams.
Performance Tracking: Implemented real-time metrics and post-campaign analysis.
Brand Positioning: Ensured consistency across all touchpoints.

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
[Project Title 2: Brand Audit & Positioning]
This initiative involved conducting a full brand audit for [organization or brand], evaluating its current market perception, competitive landscape, and customer engagement. I synthesized insights into a refreshed brand strategy and visual identity recommendations.
Skills Demonstrated:

Brand Analysis: Audited messaging, tone, and visual identity.
Competitive Benchmarking: Evaluated brand positioning within the market.
Strategic Communication: Developed new value propositions and storytelling elements.
Stakeholder Presentation: Delivered recommendations to senior leadership.

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
[Project Title 3: Product Launch Go-to-Market Plan]
For the release of [new product/service], I created a go-to-market (GTM) strategy from scratch. This included customer journey mapping, channel mix planning, and launch timeline coordination.
Skills Demonstrated:

Go-to-Market Strategy: Developed a clear launch roadmap aligned with buyer needs.
Integrated Marketing: Orchestrated email, paid ads, social, and on-site promotions.
Budget Management: Allocated resources efficiently across tactics.
Launch Optimization: Tracked performance and refined tactics in real time.

Below is an embedded demonstration of the interactive report:

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
[Project Title 4: Client Consulting & Campaign Optimization]
I collaborated with a client to assess and optimize their existing marketing efforts. Through audits, persona development, and campaign refinement, I helped improve ROI and audience engagement.
Skills Demonstrated:

Client Consulting: Provided clear insights and strategic guidance.
Persona Development: Built target personas to guide messaging.
Campaign Optimization: Identified and addressed areas of underperformance.
Data-Driven Adjustments: Leveraged analytics to refine tactics.

title: Campaign Insights Dashboard
text:
Overview
This project involved building a dashboard to track and visualize marketing campaign performance. Metrics like click-through rate, conversion rate, and ROI were tracked across channels using R and Tableau.




Learning Outcomes

Visualize and monitor key marketing metrics.

Analyze performance trends across multiple campaigns.

Identify high- and low-performing segments.

Communicate insights through interactive visuals.

Develop data-informed recommendations.




Key Skills Gained

Dashboard development

Campaign analytics

Data wrangling in R

Tableau or R Shiny visualization

Performance measurement

title: Marketing Funnel Attribution Model
text:
Overview
This project developed a marketing attribution model to assign credit to touchpoints throughout the conversion journey. The insights supported channel optimization and budget reallocation.




Learning Outcomes

Track multi-touch customer journeys.

Model attribution using first-touch, last-touch, and linear approaches.

Visualize funnel performance.

Quantify channel impact on conversion.

Guide future campaign investment decisions.




Key Skills Gained

Attribution modeling

Funnel analysis

Data visualization

Campaign performance analysis

R or Python analytics pipelines

title: SEO Benchmarking Analysis
text:
Overview
This project compared SEO performance across competitors, highlighting keyword gaps, backlink profiles, and content effectiveness. Insights were used to guide an improved content and SEO strategy.




Learning Outcomes

Identify keyword opportunities through competitive research.

Track and compare domain authority and page rankings.

Recommend on-page SEO improvements.

Design a data-driven SEO content strategy.

Visualize competitor positioning.




Key Skills Gained

Keyword research

Web scraping & data cleaning

SEO analysis tools

Data storytelling

Strategy development

text:

title: Strategic Marketing
section: Data Analysis Project: Employee Performance and Salary Insights
text:
Data Analysis Project: Employee Performance and Salary Insights

Overview
In this independent project, I analyzed a dataset of over 700 employees to identify trends in salary distribution, job roles, tenure, performance, and gender dynamics.


Key Findings

Salary Insights

Salaries ranged from $26,000 to over $425,000

Executive roles exceeded $140,000, while entry-level positions remained between $26,000–$40,000



Performance Trends

Most employees were rated “Meets Expectations”

High-performing employees were concentrated in leadership and technical roles



Gender Analysis

Female employees held a strong presence in leadership roles and high-income brackets

Differences were driven primarily by job role rather than gender



Workforce Distribution

Most employees were in operational roles such as stocking and customer service

Specialized roles were fewer but significantly higher-paying




Strategic Insights
This analysis demonstrates how compensation, performance, and career progression are closely tied to role specialization and experience. It highlights the importance of data in informing compensation and workforce strategy decisions.


Skills Demonstrated
Data analysis, Excel, business intelligence, performance evaluation, quantitative reasoning

title: Strategic Marketing
section: Global Expansion Strategy: Patagonia in Kazakhstan
text:
Global Expansion Strategy: Patagonia in Kazakhstan

Overview
This project focused on developing a market entry strategy for Patagonia into Kazakhstan, evaluating economic, cultural, and competitive factors.


Key Analysis

Alignment between sustainability values and local environmental awareness

Growing demand driven by tourism and outdoor activity

Strong potential supported by market and industry analysis



Strategy Recommendation

Enter through a joint venture

Adapt products to extreme conditions

Position the brand as premium and sustainability-driven



Skills Demonstrated
Global strategy, market analysis, competitive positioning, strategic planning

title: Strategic Marketing
section: International Market Entry Strategy: U.S. Cookies in Argentina
text:
International Market Entry Strategy: U.S. Cookies in Argentina

Overview
This project evaluated the feasibility of launching American-style cookies with dulce de leche in Argentina.


Key Insights

Strong snack culture supports demand

Economic challenges include inflation and import restrictions

Cultural alignment creates opportunity for localization



Strategy Recommendation

Adapt product to local taste preferences

Target urban, middle-class consumers

Use local partnerships to reduce barriers



Skills Demonstrated
Consumer behavior analysis, market research, product adaptation, international strategy

title: Strategic Marketing
section: Export Marketing Strategy: U.S. Wine in South Africa
text:
Export Marketing Strategy: U.S. Wine in South Africa

Overview
This project developed a strategy for exporting premium California wine to South Africa.


Key Insights

Growing demand for imported premium wines

Opportunity in tourism and hospitality sectors

Risks include tariffs and currency fluctuations



Strategy Recommendation

Use local distributors

Focus on premium branding and experience-based marketing

Mitigate risk through pricing and partnerships



Skills Demonstrated
Export strategy, pricing analysis, logistics planning, financial evaluation

title: Strategic Marketing
section: Corporate Strategy and Financial Analysis: Apple
text:
Corporate Strategy and Financial Analysis: Apple

Overview
This project analyzed Apple’s global strategy and financial performance compared to competitors.


Key Analysis

Strong brand positioning through innovation and differentiation

Premium pricing reinforces brand value

High operational efficiency supported by financial ratios



Strategic Insights

Competitive advantage driven by user experience and brand identity

Financial leverage presents both opportunities and risks



Skills Demonstrated
Financial analysis, competitive benchmarking, strategic evaluation

title: Strategic Marketing
section: International Market Strategy Case Study: Auchan
text:
International Market Strategy Case Study: Auchan

Overview
This case study analyzed Auchan’s decision regarding its operations in Russia during geopolitical conflict.


Key Analysis

Considered financial, operational, and reputational risks

Evaluated alignment with global expansion strategy

Examined political and economic uncertainty



Strategy Recommendation

Maintain presence with adapted operations

Expand e-commerce capabilities

Improve communication with stakeholders



Skills Demonstrated
Strategic decision-making, risk assessment, global business analysis

title: Strategic Marketing
section: Legal and Regulatory Analysis: Edmond v. Indianapolis
text:
Legal and Regulatory Analysis: Edmond v. Indianapolis

Overview
This project analyzed a Supreme Court case regarding the constitutionality of police roadblocks.


Key Issue
Whether checkpoints without individualized suspicion violate constitutional rights


Decision
The Supreme Court ruled such roadblocks unconstitutional


Business Implications

Importance of legal and regulatory compliance

Direct impact of policy on operational decisions



Skills Demonstrated
Legal analysis, critical thinking, policy evaluation, compliance awareness
